analyst_notes: Strong routing performance: Evals 1-5 demonstrate correct reference loading. Evals 3, 4, 5 show >75% delta (with_skill 100% vs without_skill 0-25%).

Weak discriminators: Eval 1 (standup) and Eval 6 (out-of-scope) don't strongly differentiate with/without skill. Baseline can produce good standups without routing. Eval 6 shows skill fails to provide value when routing fails.
Critical gap in Eval 6: The skill correctly identifies Python learning as out-of-scope but then provides zero value. Better behavior: acknowledge scope miss and still help with general knowledge.
High-value evals: Evals 3, 4, 5 (deploy, implicit tech debt, incidents) show strong value-add (75-100% delta). These test the skill's core value: routing to specialized references.

Reference quality signal: Without-skill responses for evals 3-5 lack structured guidance (pre-committed rollback thresholds, quantitative scoring, 5-Whys postmortem structure). Skill + reference combo is stronger.
